questions.question: Explain the purpose of the SQLiteOpenHelper class in Android development and describe the two primary methods you must override.
keyPoints: Manages database creation and version management.
Override onCreate(SQLiteDatabase db) to define the initial schema and create tables.
Override onUpgrade(SQLiteDatabase db, int oldVersion, int newVersion) to handle schema upgrades.

questions.question: Describe the difference between rawQuery() and query() methods on an SQLiteDatabase object.
keyPoints: rawQuery() executes a pre-written SQL SELECT statement and returns a Cursor.
query() is a helper method that builds a safe, parameterized query from clauses.
query() protects against SQL injection; rawQuery() is prone to injection if misused.
Use rawQuery for complex joins; use query() for standard safe SELECT operations.

questions.question: List two key advantages of using SQLite for data storage in Android compared to SharedPreferences.
keyPoints: SQLite provides full relational database structure with complex queries and relationships.
It is efficient for storing large amounts of structured data, whereas SharedPreferences is best for simple key-value pairs.
